label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out if an 8x8 chessboard with two diagonally opposite corners removed can be completely tiled with dominoes. Each domino covers two adjacent squares. So 7 steps is odd, so moving from corner to adjacent corner is one step, so color flips. But moving from top-left to bottom-right is moving 7 squares right and 7 down. But each square is adjacent, so the color alternates. Wait, actually, the color of square (row, column) can be determined by (row + column) mod 2. If even, it's one color; if odd, the other. So top-left corner is (1,1): 1+1=2, which is even, say black. The bottom-right corner is (8,8): 8+8=16, which is even. So same color. So removing both would leave us with 32 - 2 = 30 of that color, and 32 of the other. So total squares: 30 + 32 = 62, which matches. Now, each domino covers one black and one white square.
